_meta.description: Compositional strategic litmus tests. Cases reference shared hands/histories/categories instead of inlining; families expand a template across a matrix to generate multiple cases. The runner emits a flat report so the composition is invisible at read time.

kinds.single: One state, one edge, one threshold. Default kind.
kinds.pair_diff: Two states' edge probabilities should be within max_abs_diff. Symmetry checks.
kinds.monotonic: N states in order; probabilities monotonic increasing or decreasing.
kinds.exists: Like 'single' with acceptable_min — grid-slot integrity checks.

case_fields.name: Required. Human-readable label.
case_fields.expect: Tolerance object — overrides category default. Fields: acceptable_max, acceptable_min, max_abs_diff, direction.
case_fields.desired: Required. What good play looks like.
case_fields.diagnosis_if_violated: Required. What a failure tells us.

family_fields.name_template: Used to generate per-instance names. Substitutions: {hand}, {hands}, {history}, {edge}.
family_fields.matrix: Object mapping field name to list of values. Cartesian product expanded into cases.
family_fields.All other fields: Same as case_fields. Anything in matrix is filled in per-instance.

99.name: Pocket Nines — flops full house on 9c4s4hTs2c (9s/9h avoid the 9c on board)

77.name: Pocket Sevens — flops set on K72r (7s/7h avoid the 7d on board)

22.name: Pocket Deuces — flops set on K72r (2s/2h avoid the 2c on board)

bb_first_raised_K72r.desc: BB first to act, single-raised pot, dry rainbow K-7-2 board. Default 100bb stacks → SPR ≈ 24 (Deep bucket).

bb_3bet_pot_K72r.desc: BB first to act, 3-bet pot, K-7-2 rainbow. SB opens (+4 → stake 5), BB 3-bets (+12 → stake 14), SB calls (+9 → stake 14). Pot 28, eff 186 → SPR ≈ 6.6 (Mid). RAISE/CALL amounts are chip deltas, not absolute targets — see crates/gameplay/src/game.rs:416 (`bet` adds to stake).

bb_4bet_pot_K72r.desc: BB first to act, 4-bet pot, K-7-2 rainbow. SB +4 → 5; BB +12 → 14; SB +36 → 41; BB +27 → 41. Pot 82, eff 159 → SPR ≈ 1.9 (Low).

bb_5bet_pot_K72r.desc: BB first to act, 5-bet pot, K-7-2 rainbow. SB +4; BB +12; SB +36; BB +108 → stake 122; SB +81 → 122. Pot 244, eff 78 → SPR ≈ 0.32 (Committed). Rare in real play but in-distribution for the SPR-keyed infoset.

bb_first_raised_8c8d2s_paired.desc: BB first to act, raised pot, low dry board (good for set/overpair tests).

bb_first_limped_AKQss.desc: BB first to act, limped pot, AKQ all spades (monotone).

bb_first_raised_AKQ_rainbow.desc: BB first to act, raised pot, AKQ rainbow.

sb_facing_check_KTs2s7d.desc: SB to act on turn after preflop raise, flop check-check.

bb_first_checkdown_Js9s2s7d4s.desc: BB first to act on river, single-raised pot, fully checked-down (4-flush spade runout).

bb_facing_big_bet_AsKcQh8s4d.desc: BB facing SB pot-sized river bet on AKQT4 board (mostly bricked).

bb_facing_big_bet_9c4s4hTs2c_paired.desc: BB facing SB pot bet on 9-4-4-T-2 paired runout.

bb_facing_jam_Kd7s2c5d9h.desc: BB facing SB river jam on dry K-7-2-5-9 runout.

preflop_overjam.description: Strong defending hands collapsing to all-in instead of 3-betting at 100bb HU. Failure means action grid has a cliff between max non-jam raise and all-in, OR postflop EV makes shoving look artificially good.

preflop_premium_control.description: Hands that should stay aggressive but never need to jam (AKs, AA). Failure means we regressed something previously correct.

preflop_weak_rags.description: Worst HU starting hands (72o, 83o, 62o, ...). Real GTO opens >95% of hands; >50% fold here is a stuck-bucket signature.

suited_offsuit_symmetry.description: Same rank pair, suited and offsuit, should play similarly. Asymmetry diagnoses the postflop abstraction losing suit-specific equity-realization.

rank_monotonicity.description: Across an ordered hand-strength sequence, the strategy should move monotonically. Non-monotonicity is the signature of a bucket-specific stuck state.

flop_value.description: Strong made hands on appropriate boards (TPTK, set, flush) should not fold/check at structurally implausible rates.

flop_air.description: Air on bad boards should check, not over-bet.

turn_value.description: Strong turn hands (nut flush, set) should bet with non-trivial frequency.

river_value.description: Strong rivered hands (nut flush, full house) should bet/raise; never fold to a single bet.

river_air.description: Air vs significant river bet should fold.

river_invariant.description: TPTK vs all-in must call (no fold equity to extract by folding).

structural_grid.description: Each grid slot should appear at non-trivial frequency somewhere. A slot used at <2% everywhere is dead and should be retired in v3.

structural_grid_v3.description: Grid v3 dropped the SPR axis from the InfoSet key; menus are now keyed only on (street, depth). This category is reserved for v3-specific structural checks; populate as new patterns emerge from v3 training data.

_postflop_holes_note: Postflop scenarios pair a hole-card hand with a board-specific history. The history's `_seen` field carries the board (after `~`). The runner combines hand.cards + ' ~ ' + board to form the API's `seen` parameter.

tests.name: QQ overpair on K72r: not pure-check first-to-act
tests.desired: QQ on K-high single-raised pot first-to-act: K beats Q so this is awkward, but pure-check (>95%) leaves value on the table and pure-bet (>80%) is over-aggressive on a board where villain can have K-x. Want a real mix — bet across some size at >20% frequency. (Fold is not in the legal choice set when first-to-act with no bet facing; the prior version of this test queried `F` against a no-fold cell and trivially passed.)
tests.diagnosis_if_violated: Pure check with QQ on K72r means no value-bet line at all. Postflop abstraction may be collapsing QQ into a hand class that never c-bets dry boards.

tests.name: Bet-size diversity: 1:4 (quarter-pot) lives somewhere on flop
tests.desired: 1:4 should appear at >2% in at least some flop spots. Canonical TPTK on dry board is the most likely spot for small probe bets.
tests.diagnosis_if_violated: 1:4 dead at TPTK suggests the slot is dead generally. Sample more flop spots before retiring it in v3.

tests.name: Bet-size diversity: 3:1 (overbet) lives somewhere postflop
tests.desired: AKo BB-defending should use 3:1 at >5% — it's the new recommended 4-bet size in this spot.
tests.diagnosis_if_violated: 3:1 below 5% on AKo BB-defends-2bb means the new size from PR #155 isn't getting used; check whether it's available in the action grid or whether CFR routes around it.

families.desired: Real HU GTO: 0-5% jam at 100bb. Hands defend by 3-betting to 1.5x-3x pot, not by jamming.
families.diagnosis_if_violated: Above the 30% category threshold: structural over-jam from action-grid `2:1 → !` cliff or postflop EV pessimism. Mitigations: bigger non-jam raise size, better postflop abstraction, or `bias_shove=0` on next retrain.

families.desired: Control. AKs and AA were always correct pre-fix; should stay at near-zero jam.
families.diagnosis_if_violated: Regression. AKs/AA collapsing to all-in means a previously-fine spot broke.

families.desired: Suited and offsuit versions of the same rank-pair should play similarly. Real GTO: identical strategies (both ~0% jam at these depths).
families.diagnosis_if_violated: Big gap = postflop abstraction is losing suit-specific equity-realization information. AKs has flush-draw paths AKo doesn't. If clusters collapse them, AKo's polarized lines (jam) win regret share. Fix: refine postflop clustering or higher K.

families.desired: Real HU GTO opens >95% from SB. Weak rags can mix open/fold but should rarely fold pure.
families.diagnosis_if_violated: Stuck-fold via Linear-CFR's positive-regret-undiscounted weighting. F edge accumulates more regret than competing edges can overcome. Consider DCFR with negative-regret discount.

families.desired: Jam frequency should increase from AA (weakest reason to jam) to JJ (most reason to take immediate fold equity). Real GTO has all near 0%, but the GRADIENT should hold.
families.diagnosis_if_violated: Non-monotonic = bucket-specific equilibrium quirk. If AA jams more than KK, AA's payoff calculation is broken.

families.desired: Worst hand folds most; better hands fold less. Linear gradient of fold rate.
families.diagnosis_if_violated: Non-monotonic gradient (e.g., 72o folds 50% but 73o folds 0%) is a bucket-specific stuck-fold artifact.

families.name_template: Flop air on monotone board: must check
families.desired: Bottom-of-range hand on monotone high-card flop is a classic give-up spot.
families.diagnosis_if_violated: Below 70% check means bot is over-bluffing the worst spots.

families.name_template: Flop air on draw-heavy board: must check
families.desired: Trash hand on AKQ rainbow with no equity should rarely bet.

families.name_template: {hand} Flop TPTK in raised pot: not pure-check first-to-act
families.desired: TPTK on a dry K-high flop first-to-act should bet a meaningful share of the time. Pure check (>95%) leaves value on the table; pure bet (<5% check) means no slowplay. Want a real mix. (Fold is not in the legal choice set when first-to-act with no bet facing; the prior version queried `F` against a no-fold cell and trivially passed.)
families.diagnosis_if_violated: Pure check with TPTK on K72r means no c-bet line at all. Likely the postflop abstraction is averaging TPTK into a check-call cluster with weaker hands.

families.name_template: Flop set in raised pot: not pure-check
families.desired: Overpair on low dry board: bet/check is a valid mix. Pure check (>95%) means no value-bet line; pure bet (<10% check) means no slowplay. Want something in between.

families.name_template: River nut flush in checked-down pot: bet often
families.desired: BB rivered nut flush after a checked-down street. Should bet across some size at >50% frequency. Pure check is correct only as deception in tournaments at very deep stacks; in cash HU, betting dominates.
families.diagnosis_if_violated: Pure check with the nuts on river leaves money on the table. The cluster might be averaging across hands of varying strength, but pure-check still means structural under-betting.

families.name_template: River full house facing big bet: never fold
families.desired: Hero has nines full of fours (9s9h on 9c4s4hTs2c). Folding a full house to a single bet is the cardinal river sin.
families.diagnosis_if_violated: If a full house folds: postflop abstraction is collapsing strong hands into the same cluster as bluff-catchers, losing all signal.

families.name_template: River air vs big bet: high fold
families.desired: Total air vs big river bet on a board where nothing improved should fold a clear majority.

families.name_template: River TPTK vs all-in: must call
families.desired: TPTK on dry blank runout vs jam beats most of villain's value range; calling station is correct since fold-equity is zero post-jam.

families.name_template: Turn nut flush in raised pot: bet often
families.desired: SB rivered the nut flush on the turn (KsTs2s7d, AsKs in hand). Should bet across some size at >40% frequency. Pure check is wrong.
